entry.title: So wenden Sie sich an das SAP Education - Learning-Hub-Team um Unterstützung zu erhalten
entry.content:
** This blog was in collaboration/translation with my German speaking colleague Rebecca Dolan

Benötigen Sie Unterstützung vom Learning Hub/Schulungsteam erhalten und wissen nicht, wo Sie anfangen sollen? Dann ist dieser Blog für Sie…  

Hier zeige ich Ihnen die Schritte, um das SAP Learning Support Center zu kontaktieren. Die Support-Mitarbeiter sind Montag bis Freitag, rund um die Uhr verfügbar, und die meisten Anfragen werden innerhalb von 24 Stunden beantwortet. 

* Klicken Sie hier, wenn Sie dies auf Englisch oder Spanisch lesen möchten

 

In der Support-Plattform finden Sie auch häufig gestellte Fragen zu folgenden Themen:  

  • Allgemeine Support-Themen  
  • Praxissysteme  
  • Themen von Schulungs-Reseller-Partnern  
  • Lern- und Zertifizierungsberatung   
  • Kursbuchung und Bezahlung   
  • Und Analyse-Dashboard 

 Anlegen eines Falls  

Wenn Sie feststellen, dass Sie auf der Supportplattform nicht die gewünschten Informationen finden, können Sie einen Fall anlegen, um Unterstützung mit Ihrer Anfrage zu erhalten.   

Dazu müssen Sie sich lediglich mit Ihrer Universal ID anmelden und ein Ticket erstellen. 

Auf der SAP-Learning-Hub-Website:  

  1. Klicken Sie auf das Fragezeichen (?) rechts oben auf der Seite.
  2. Auf der Help-Center-Seite wählen Sie dann auf die Registerkarte „Contact Us“, und klicken dann auf „Request Technical Assistance“. 
  3. Klicken Sie dann auf „Need More Help“. 

     

  4. Melden Sie sich mit Ihrer Universal ID an, und klicken Sie dann auf "Create Case". 

 Auf der SAP-Learning-Website:  

  1. Scrollen Sie nach unten zum Ende der Seite, und klicken Sie unter „Support and Contact“ auf „Get Support“. 
  2. Nun werden Sie auf die User-Support-Center Webseite weitergeleitet. Dort finden Sie die FAQs zur Selbsthilfe und detaillierte Leitfäden.  
  3. Wenn der erforderliche Support nicht in den FAQs gefunden wird, scrollen Sie nach unten, und klicken auf das „Need Help“ Symbol. Sie werden dann zur nächsten Seite weitergeleitet, um ein Ticket zu erstellen.  
  4. Klicken Sie "Create Case". 

     

Darüber hinaus können Sie den direkten Link zum User-Support-Center verwenden.  

Um den Status Ihres Tickets zu sehen, klicken Sie auf den Refresh-Button, um den Status ihres Tickets zu aktualisieren. 

Worin können Supportmitarbeiter Sie unterstützen?  

Das Schulungsteam kann Sie unterstützen, wenn Sie Informationen zu Kursen, Zertifizierungsinhalten und Learning Journeys erhalten möchten. Außerdem kann es Sie beraten, um zu verstehen, welche Schulungen erforderlich sind, um Ihre Ziele zu erreichen.  

Weitere Leistungen sind:   

  • Fragen zur Zertifizierung, einschließlich Ratschläge zur korrekten UID-Einrichtung und wie Sie auf Ihre Badges zugreifen können.   
  • Probleme im Zusammenhang mit Informations-, Liefer- und Nachsorgeleistungen von Unterrichtsräumen mit Kursleiter.  
  • Buchungen und abrechnungsbezogene Probleme werden in der Regel von einem anderen internen Team bearbeitet, aber das Learning-Team kann sich einbringen, um die Genehmigung für kostenlose Umbuchungen und ähnliche Szenarien zu erteilen, da diese häufig mit Kundenreklamationen usw. zusammenhängen.  
  • Visabriefe.  
  • Bereitstellen von Lieferanteninformationen auf Anforderung. 

Fordern Sie Unterstützung in einer anderen Sprache an.  

Wenn Sie möchten, dass das Schulungsteam Sie in Ihrer bevorzugten Sprache unterstützt, können Sie die Sprache auswählen, nachdem Sie die Beschreibung hinzugefügt haben. Sie müssen das Land auswählen, in dem Sie sich befinden, und zwischen Deutsch, Englisch, Spanisch, Französisch und Japanisch wählen. 

Zusätzlich empfehlen wir Ihnen den Blog  SAP Certifications and UID...all you need to know! Um mehr zum Them SAP-Zertifizierungen und UID zu erfahren. 

Der folgende Wissensdatenbankartikel kann auch hilfreich sein: 2404504 - Hilfe zum SAP Learning Hub oder SAP Training Center 

Danke fürs Lesen!

entry.title: 32-bit CR.NET support question
entry.content:
We are currently using the 32-bit CR .NET Runtime in our application. According to this SAP documentation,  https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354088411.html, the 32-bit CR .NET Runtime will be discontinued after December 2025. Could you clarify what "discontinued" means in this context? Will it still be available for use? Additionally, how will this affect   our application that have already been shipped to customers earlier and are still in use?

From the same page, it appears that the overall end of support for Crystal Reports is 2027. Do you have any recommendations for a migration path once Crystal Reports reaches its end of life?

entry.title: Cómo comunicarse con el equipo de SAP Education - Learning Hub para obtener ayuda
entry.content:
¿Está buscando obtener ayuda del SAP Education/Learning Hub y no sabe por dónde empezar? Entonces este blog es para usted... 

Aquí compartiré los pasos que debes seguir para interactuar con el área de soporte; los agentes están disponibles las 24 horas del día, de lunes a viernes, y la mayoría de las consultas se responden dentro de las 24 horas. 

* Haga click aqui si desea leer este blog en Alemán o en Inglés

En  la plataforma de soporte también podrás encontrar preguntas frecuentes relacionadas con: 

  • Temas de soporte general 
  • Sistemas de práctica 
  • Temas de capacitación para socios revendedores 
  • Asesoramiento sobre aprendizaje y certificación  
  • Reserva y pago de cursos  
  • Y panel de análisis 

Cómo crear un caso 

Si la información que está buscando no está en la plataforma, puede crear un caso para obtener ayuda sobre su consulta específica.  

Todo lo que necesita hacer es iniciar sesión con su Universal ID y crear un ticket. 

Desde el sitio SAP Learning Hub : 

  1. Debe hacer clic en el ícono del signo de interrogación (?) que se encuentra en la parte superior derecha de la página. 
  2. En la página del Centro de ayuda, haga clic en el botón “Contáctenos” y seleccione “Solicitar asistencia técnica”
     
  3. Haga clic en "¿Necesita más ayuda?" 
  4. Inicie sesión con su ID Universal y luego deberá hacer clic en "Crear caso" 

Desde el sitio de aprendizaje de SAP : 

  1. Desplácese hasta la parte inferior de la página y haga clic en “Obtener soporte”, que se encuentra en Soporte y contacto. 
  2. Será dirigido a la siguiente página, la página del Centro de soporte al usuario, donde podrá encontrar las preguntas frecuentes de autoayuda y guías detalladas. 
  3. Si no encuentra la ayuda que necesita en las preguntas frecuentes, desplácese hacia abajo y haga clic en el ícono "¿Necesita ayuda?" y será dirigido a la página siguiente para generar un ticket. 
  4. Haga clic en el botón “Crear caso”. 

Además, puede utilizar el enlace directo al Centro de soporte al usuario . 

Para ver la actualización de su ticket: haga clic en el botón Actualizar antes de encontrar la actualización de su caso/ticket. 

¿En qué pueden ayudarle los agentes de soporte? 

El Equipo de Aprendizaje puede ayudarle si desea obtener información sobre cursos, contenido de certificación y rutas de aprendizaje. También pueden asesorarle para que comprenda qué aprendizaje es necesario para lograr sus objetivos. 

Otros servicios incluyen:  

  • Preguntas de primer nivel sobre certificación, incluido asesoramiento sobre la configuración correcta del UID y cómo acceder a sus credenciales.  
  • Cuestiones relacionadas con la información, la prestación y el servicio posterior a las clases dirigidas por instructores. 
  • Los problemas relacionados con las reservas y la facturación generalmente los maneja otro equipo interno, pero el equipo de aprendizaje puede involucrarse para dar aprobación para cualquier nueva reserva gratuita y situaciones similares, ya que a menudo están relacionadas con quejas de los clientes, etc. 
  • Cartas de visa. 
  • Proporcionar información de proveedores a pedido. 

Solicitar soporte en un idioma diferente. 

Si desea que el equipo de aprendizaje lo ayude en su idioma preferido, puede seleccionar el idioma una vez que haya agregado la descripción, deberá elegir el país en el que se encuentra y seleccionar entre: Alemán, Inglés, Español, Francés y Japonés. 

También nos gustaría compartir este blog para que puedas aprender más sobre las certificaciones SAP y UID... ¡todo lo que necesitas saber! Y este KBA también puede ser útil:  2404504 - Cómo obtener ayuda con SAP Learning Hub o SAP Training Center 

¡Gracias por leer!

entry.title: Global benefits provisioning job didn't work for persons who is terminated and newly hired
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a scenario where employee is terminated and newly hired in a different country, so in this case person ID remain same but User ID changes however when I run the benefits master data changes provisioning job then the log shows "Processed 0 users" in Execution Manager Dashboard which means that the system is unable to recognise this user based on the person ID provided in the job parameters.

In a different scenario, when when we run the benefit master data changes Job for Global assignment person ID's, the system successfully processed the users to validate the eligibility criteria for benefit plan enrolment.

The common factor in both the scenarios is person ID remain same and user id changes. So what is the criteria in the first case why system does not process the person when terminated and newly hired.

Please let me know if anyone is experienced in Benefits module and came across this situation and how to fix it.

Thanks,

Thrilleswar.

entry.title: Re: Python samples in ASE did not work and fail with an I...
entry.content:
Hi,

This actually isn't a typo, but documentation that hasn't been updated for newer versions of the module.

I've created a CR or change request for these Python pages. I've found 3 pages so far.

The python modules are built with specific python versions.
Windows
%SYBASE%\%SYBASE_OCS%\python\pythonXX_64
All other platforms
$SYBASE/$SYBASE_OCS/python/pythonXX_64r

XX will be the python version that the module is built for in that directory.
So python 3.11 would use python311_64r

Hope that helps,
Ryan

entry.title: SAP Activate for RISE with SAP: Roadmap Updates & New Content!
entry.content:
My original Blog Post about SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition has gotten over 17,000 views - thank you all so much for your interest! But since then, the SAP Activate team has made some significant updates to the SAP Activate roadmap for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and a lot of new content has been released. 

In the following blog I want to highlight the following updates, that we have made since the publication of that last blog. We made more changes throughout the years and weeks since we publish on a bi-weekly release, but these are the most important and recent updates

  • Clean Core Strategy 
  • Selective Data Transition Path 
  • SAP Cloud ALM 
  • SAP Signavio 
  • SAP LeanIX
  • Release Notes 

Clean Core Strategy 

A key change that customers will notice is the introduction of the Clean Core Strategy, announced at the beginning of the year. The 'clean core' is a concept designed to achieve modern, flexible, and cloud-compliant ERPs. For an up-to-date ERP, customers achieve a clean core by integrating and extending the system in a cloud-compliant way, while governing master data and business processes. This approach ensures that when it comes time to upgrade a system, changes can be implemented without significant manual efforts to test and adapt existing structures. 

    Figure 1: Criteria for a Clean Core

SAP Activate offers advice for customers who want to keep their ERP systems clean from unnecessary changes. This way, their organization can take advantage of the latest release with minimal modifications and extensions & customizations that work well in the cloud. SAP Activate contains recommended tasks in the Clean Core Success Plan. It is available as a Tag that you can access on the Content tab. One of the main steps is to set up a governance for clean core by creating the Solution Standardization Board in Prepare phase and regularly performing governance activities, including capturing the action plan in dedicated runbook project in SAP Cloud ALM to track your progress towards clean core objectives. 

To get you up to speed on Clean Core, check out these resources: 

    • ERP Clean Core Strategy
    • SAP Activate Minute for RISE with SAP Clean Core
    • Clean Core related enhancements in the Solution Standardization Board for RISE with SAP.
    • Enhanced RISE with SAP Methodology with clean core quality checks
    • Learn here how to setup a dedicated runbook in SAP Cloud ALM

Selective Data Transition

Another key change that was made is that we added a new transition scenario in the SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. The Selective Data Transition.

With the Selective Data Transition, you can migrate entire or selective historical data into SAP S/4HANA using high-speed and flexible data migration process. With a selective data transition, the source system is an SAP ERP system, while the target will be the SAP S/4HANA system, which can be an empty shell, a newly implemented, or an already productive system. The migration typically requires minimal downtime and can be executed either in a single step or in waves. It offers a balanced blend of the New Implementation and System Conversion approaches, combining their advantages to provide customers greater flexibility in data transfer and harmonization when transitioning to SAP S/4HANA. 

The content for this transition path was added via a tag in the roadmap viewer, where you can find deliverables, tasks and accelerators to help you understand the transition path and how SAP can support you. 

SAP Cloud ALM 

SAP Cloud ALM enhances the Activate methodology by delivering support for project management and execution. Over the years, we have introduced numerous SAP Activate templates within SAP Cloud ALM. When initiating a project, customers can select a specific implementation roadmap to guide their transformation journey.

For instance, the new selective data transition scenario mentioned above, is now supported by a dedicated template in Cloud ALM. This template is updated bi-weekly with the latest changes, which are accessible through the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer.

When starting a new project, customers can easily select the appropriate template to start their transformation journey. (Refer to Figure 2)

Figure 2: SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation 

Additionally, customers leveraging Cloud ALM and the Business Transformation Center can unlock even greater value. The Business Transformation tool, integral to the selective data transition process, is seamlessly integrated within Cloud ALM. 

In the image below, customers can see how Cloud ALM enables seamless navigation from the project implementation tab directly to the transformation project to use Business Transformation Center, during a Lean selective data transition scenario. 

Figure 3: SAP Business Transformation Center in Cloud ALM

Learn more by watching this great enablement Lean Selective Data Transition Overview Video. 

Additional transition scenario for SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition are also available in SAP Cloud ALM and updated bi-weekly. 

SAP Signavio

SAP Signavio helps accelerate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition transformation and business process performance. SAP Signavio is a platform that allows customers to model, analyze, optimize and execute business processes. SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub facilitates collaboration and communication on a project while SAP Signavio Process Manager helps customer re-imagine, document, model and simulate business processes. 

SAP Activate contains SAP Signavio procedure notes with details on how customers can leverage the tool during their transformation journey. You can also find specific Signavio solutions such as Process Insights, Process Intelligence, Process Governance are used in various tasks. It is available in the Product section that you can access on the Content tab.

To get you up to speed on SAP Signavio in SAP Activate, check out these resources: 

  • SAP Signavio Product Tag in SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Signavio Methodology for SAP S/4HANA Transformation

SAP LeanIX

SAP LeanIX is a solution that allows customers to manage and optimize enterprise architecture. SAP LeanIX provides a 360° overview of all applications, business capabilities, and IT components, enabling customers to gain control of their expanding IT landscape and create roadmaps for major transformation initiatives. Through collaborative and democratized data collection, out-of-the-box integrations, and fast insights through reports and diagrams, SAP LeanIX enables customers to manage enterprise architecture effectively. SAP LeanIX can be beneficial throughout an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Implementation, supporting initial assessment and creating a clear overview of the existing IT landscape. 

SAP Activate contains procedure notes on SAP LeanIX in relevant tasks, with more information on how customers can leverage the tool during their transformation journey. It is available in the Product section that you can access on the Content tab.

To get you up to speed on SAP LeanIX in SAP Activate, check out these resources: 

  • SAP LeanIX Product Tag in SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP LeanIX Home Page
  • Self-Enable on SAP LeanIX 

 

Release Notes 

To help you navigate these changes, you can refer to the release notes here. You'll find a summary of what's new, updated, or changed in the key deliverable tasks, and accelerators. The implementation roadmap is updated biweekly, so please be sure to check it regularly for the latest updates.  

Stay tuned for ongoing enhancements and future updates! We're always striving to improve our content, and your feedback plays a crucial role in that process. Feel free to share your thoughts and suggestions via the Roadmap Viewer page—we’d love to hear from you!

Head over to the release blog mentioned in the first section and explore the latest updates to SAP Activate for RISE with SAP.

Thanks again for reading

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 SAP GUI and Scripting - Does not work af...
entry.content:
As it is known, Personas scripts in SAP GUI for Windows are not always running the same as they do in a browser, since the JavaScript capabilities of the Windows GUI are restricted. It is always better to consume flavors in a browser session instead of WinGUI.

With that said, if a script was working OK in the Windows GUI earlier, then it ought to work after a Windows update too. The first thing I'd suggest is trying to test the issue with the latest available WinGUI version and patch level, and if that is still not working, then create a customer support incident for component BC-FES-GUI (not BC-PER). It is the Windows GUI support team's job to help with issues related to Personas flavor consumption there.

entry.title: How to do printing of Multiple Warehouse Tasks?
entry.content:
We have a business requirement to print multiple warehouse tasks at one go. Currently, we are able to print only one task at a time in Warehouse Monitor APP - Documents - Warehouse Orders - Warehouse Tasks - Print.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to do printing of Multiple Warehouse Tasks?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community, 

I would recommend checking the APP Process Warehouse Tasks.  

You can directly choose one or multiple warehouse tasks for confirmation or cancellation. You can print a warehouse task list with this app.

To preview the warehouse task list, you can choose the Reprint Warehouse Order method under the Warehouse Order node of the Warehouse Monitor app. When you choose a warehouse task for printing, the system prints a single document for the relevant warehouse order. All warehouse tasks for this warehouse order are printed. In the default setting, the system prints the list for only open warehouse orders. If you want to print a list for a warehouse order with a different status, such as Confirmed, adjust the settings in the Output Parameter Determination app.

For further information, please check the SAP documentaiton:

Process Warehouse Tasks

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: FDA tracking with S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @Antoine ,

you ask how we tackle the issue of compliance with certifications imposed by FDA, CSA and other such authorities. The quick answer is, we do not. Unless specified, it is not typically software providers' obligation to meet those certification requirements. An 'entity' in regulated industries may however be required to get audited for their processes, practices and IT infrastructure and to ensure they comply with that industry's regulatory requirements. Where processes, etc., may be deemed insufficient, they, the 'entity' may be required to adapt their processes, etc., accordingly. 

SAP provides for adaptability of their software through configuration and extension capabilities. SAP also provides for best practices across many lines of business, industries, and countries. However, it is always the software using entity that has to maintain its compliance with regulations, not the supplier. 

SAP does comply with an array of regulatory requirements across multiple countries. With which exactly and in respect to what products you can find on the Compliance pages of sap.com, here.

If this response was helpful to you, please mark it accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: End of Year Celebration: SAP Inside Track 2024 - Transforming with SAP S/4HANA and RISE
entry.content:
Why This Event is a Must-Attend

At the heart of this year’s event are two compelling sessions that will provide you with real-world insights and actionable takeaways. These presentations are designed not only to highlight the success stories of our customers but also to give you the tools and strategies you need to make your own transformation smoother and more successful.

Session 1:S/4HANA RISE at WEL Networks: A Partner-Led Brownfield Transformation

For the first time ever, we’ll have Chris Puddle from WEL Networks and Paul Brown, Solution Architect at Deloitte, take the stage to share the story of their successful Brownfield transformation from SAP ECC to S/4HANA with RISE with SAP. This session will cover the entire journey—from project initiation and timelines to the real challenges and key milestones that helped them succeed.

You’ll learn:

  • How WEL Networks partnered with Deloitte to navigate the complexities of the transformation process.
  • What it means to undergo a Brownfield transformation in the context of S/4HANA and why it's crucial for those still on ECC.
  • Key insights, tips, and “gotchas” that can save you time and resources as you prepare for your own move to S/4HANA.

This session is perfect for anyone involved in or planning to embark on an S/4HANA transformation, especially as we approach the 2027 deadline for ECC migration.

Session 2: Unlock the Full Potential of Your GROW Entitlements: Maximize SAP S/4HANA Cloud Benefits

If you’re already using SAP S/4HANA Cloud or are considering the shift, then you’ll want to hear from Grace Melhuish, Enterprise Customer Success Partner at SAP. Grace will walk you through how to maximize your GROW with SAP entitlements to fully unlock the power of S/4HANA Cloud.

This session will focus on:

  • Continuous Quality Checks and Cloud ALM, helping you ensure your system is running smoothly and optimally at every stage.
  • How to leverage your SAP investment to drive business transformation, not just on the technical side, but in delivering real business value.
  • Tips on ensuring long-term success in the cloud and how you can utilize the full suite of services available to you.

Whether you’re just starting out with S/4HANA Cloud or looking to enhance your existing setup, this session will provide valuable insights into how to get the most out of your cloud investment.

Event Details:

📅Date: 4th December 2024
⏰Time: 4:30 PM onwards
📍Location: SAP Building, Queen Street

This is a free event, and we encourage you to bring your colleagues, friends, and peers who are also looking to accelerate their SAP journey. It’s a fantastic opportunity to connect with the SAP community, learn from experts, and set yourself up for success in 2025.

Register Your Interest Don’t wait—spaces are filling up fast! ⏳

A Huge Thank You to Our Community Before we dive into the event itself, we want to take a moment to thank you—our SAP community—for your incredible support over the past year. The success of our SAP Inside Track events wouldn’t be possible without your enthusiasm, feedback, and passion for learning and growing together.

We’ve listened to your feedback from our previous events and have made some exciting changes to this year’s format to ensure even more value and diversity. By moving away from a sole focus on BTP (Business Technology Platform), we’ve expanded our content to reach a broader audience and to better serve the needs of everyone on their SAP journey.

Let’s Close 2024 with a Bang! This event is the perfect opportunity to gather fresh insights, share experiences, and finish the year with actionable takeaways that will set you up for a successful 2025. We’re excited to welcome WEL Networks, Deloitte, and SAP experts to share their experiences, and we hope you’ll join us for a memorable end-of-year celebration.

Spread the word—this is an event you don’t want to miss!

See you on December 4th!

#SAPInsideTrack #S4HANA #RISEwithSAP #GROWwithSAP #TransformationJourney #CustomerStory #SAPCommunity #TechInnovation #LearningTogether #CloudSuccess #DigitalTransformation #EventReminder #FinishStrong #EndOfYearCelebration @jpagcamaran @LuisBecker1 @GDR_ANZ @gracemelhuish @JJSingh @christoph_christophersen @Murali_Shanmugham

entry.title: Re: How to load column table data into hdb collection
entry.content:
You did not write what issues you faced, but for me to get it working I needed to use

 

insert into "myCollection" (SELECT 'myvalue' AS "key" FROM dummy FOR JSON ('arraywrap'='no'));

 

1/ You do not need `VALUES` when inserting from `SELECT`: https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-json-document-store-guide/insert-statement#examples

2/ The result of your `SELECT` is 

[{"key":"myvalue"}]

which cannot be inserted per documentation:

It is not possible to insert multiple documents with a single string, like a string that contains an array of documents.

so I used json_option_string_list 

('arraywrap'='no')

to remove `[]`.

I hope this helps.
--Vitaliy

entry.title: Providing Feedback in SAP Activate road map viewer via SAP for Me
entry.content:
Last week, my colleague @CKGurav blogged about the introduction of SAP Activate road map viewer in SAP for Me. Following up on his insights, I’d like to talk about the feedback functionality that empowers you to shape our SAP Activate methodology, tools and processes.

If you’ve ever wondered what happens to the feedback you submit, my colleague Dan @dcko  wrote a great blog explaining the process: Behind the Scenes with SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer Feedback. It’s a great overview at how your insights are transformed into meaningful updates and improvements.

Feedback functionality in SAP Activate road map viewer

Feedback is the backbone of how we improve our content. It’s not just about fixing issues reported only, it’s about understanding what works for you, identifying opportunities, and creating content to help you with your transformation journey. 

Here’s why your input is invaluable:

  • It Guides Priorities: Your insights help us focus on the content that matter most.
  • It Sparks Innovation: Feedback often uncovers ideas and opportunities we hadn’t considered, and it help us assess how you are using our content during your transformation journey. 
  • It Builds Better content: Direct input from users ensures our SAP Activate Methodology is user-friendly and impactful.

Updates View via SAP for Me 

Figure 1: SAP Activate road map viewer in SAP for Me - Feedback 

If you’ve explored the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer via SAP for Me, you may have noticed a new Feedback button that wasn’t previously there (See Figure 1). This addition provides two distinct feedback channels:

  1. SAP for Me Feedback: Use this option to share your thoughts on the overall tool experience, including navigation, usability, or additional features you’d like to see (Figure 1).
  2. Roadmap Viewer Feedback: This specific feedback option allows you to provide input on roadmaps content, accelerators, or any challenges you encounter within the Roadmap Viewer itself (Figure 2).

It’s important to understand the difference between these options so your feedback is directed to the right team for action.

See the following image, to understand where you can provide feedback for SAP Activate content related topics. 

Figure 2: SAP Activate Feedback functionality 

Keep the Feedback Coming 

We continue to gather feedback from our own customer community, our SAP experts, and partners, and we follow an agile methodology to build on the previous version and continuously improve the content.

In the past, your feedback has driven meaningful improvements. Whether it was simplifying navigation, creating new tasks, or updating accelerators, your input has helped us focus on what truly matters to you, during your implementation journey. 

The feedback functionality is here to ensure your voice is heard. So, as you explore the new updates, remember that your input directly influences the evolution of our SAP Activate Methodology content.

Thank you for your ongoing support and engagement! 

We will continue to review your feedback, and we encourage to keep using this functionality and share your thoughts.

entry.title: Re: How to do you move flavour changes in SAP screen pers...
entry.content:
That must be some monster flavor if you have such a problem with export / import, and it's a strange situation to have a dump because of this.

My first suggestion would be to compress the undo history of the flavor, since if you want to get the flavor to another client or system, then you are presumably done with development and thus it is unlikely you'd need to revert changes in the flavor editor. This could reduce the number of changes significantly and avoid the dump. In addition, I would also permanently delete (not just mark as deleted) all old, unneeded versions of the flavor for the same reason.

However, if this is a recurring situation for you when you develop in a client where there is sufficient business data and then you need to send the objects to another client where your transports originate from, then this is exactly what the 'push to client' functionality is for. It would be the preferred solution long term. Here is the corresponding documentation page, explaining how to use it: Transport Manager | SAP Help Portal
Still, the above compress / deletion suggestions apply nevertheless, before moving objects.

Alternatively, you could also use the regular transport system via transaction SCC1 or SCC1N but I believe the previous methods should help to achieve your goal.

entry.title: Reading Invoice PDF from External systems in S/4 Hana
entry.content:
In this blog , i will discuss on how to read the invoice PDF generated through Outputs in SAP S/4 system from External systems.

S/4 Hana provided a standard API to read , cancel , get PDF  & complete Proforma invoice.

In order to use the API  API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV to get PDF , outputs should be configured using BRF+.

Read Operation:

Read can be used to retrieve Billing document header details based on the billing document number and item details based on billing document header and item number.

use Get Method to read the billing document details.

Cancel  Operation:

Cancel operation is used to cancel single or multiple invoices based on the request from external system or consumers.

POST HTTP method is used to cancel the billing documents. ETAG handling is required for Posting the cancellation.

For any cancellation requests IF-MATCH should be mentioned in the HTTP request, if it is missing then client throws an error response.

Get PDF:

Get PDF method is used to retrieve the PDF from S/4 system outputs.

PDF will be sent to the external system in a ASCII string format (encoded in Base64). The receiver system should convert the base 64 to binary format using a tool or API.

PDF will be encoded in base64, an encoding scheme that represents binary data in an ASCII string format before sent to receiver. 

Example to get PDF:

GET <host>/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV/GetPDF?BillingDocument=''9xxxxxxxxx'

entry.title: Re: Binding transfer problem
entry.content:
@bill_froelich 

i think i found the problem.

Inside FetchSalesOrder.js there is the following code for Fetch all the needed data:

//read items    
    clientAPI.read(servicePath, entitySet, [], query).then((result) => {

        appClientData.Items = [];

        for (let i = 0; i < result.length; i++) {
            let orderItem = result.getItem(i);
            appClientData.Items.push(orderItem);
        }

        //read texts
        entitySet = "TextSet";
        return clientAPI.read(servicePath, entitySet, [], query);

    }).then((result) => {

        appClientData.HeaderTexts = [];
        appClientData.ItemTexts = [];

        for (let i = 0; i < result.length; i++) {
            let orderText = result.getItem(i);
            if (orderText.Posnr === "") {
                appClientData.HeaderTexts.push(orderText);
            } else {
                appClientData.ItemTexts.push(orderText);
            }
        }

        //read conditions
        entitySet = "ConditionSet";
        return clientAPI.read(servicePath, entitySet, [], query);

    }).then((result) => {

        let orderCondition = [];
        for (let i = 0; i < result.length; i++) {
            orderCondition.push(result.getItem(i));
        }

        appClientData.PricesCondition = orderCondition.reduce(function (r, a) {
            r[a.Pos] = r[a.Pos] || [];
            r[a.Pos].push(a);
            return r;
        }, Object.create(null));

    }).then((result) => {
        
        clientAPI.dismissActivityIndicator();
        return clientAPI.executeAction("/App/Actions/ShowSalesOrderActions/NavToShowSalesOrder.action");

    }).catch((error) => {
        return Utility.displayErrorMessage(clientAPI.localizeText('DataReadError', [binding.OrderS]));
    });

If the clientAPI.executeAction for Navigation in inside the then( ) method of Promise the binding fail to transfer to the next page.

If I copy the executeAction outside like this:

    //read items    
    clientAPI.read(servicePath, entitySet, [], query).then((result) => {

 ..........

    }).catch((error) => {
        return Utility.displayErrorMessage(clientAPI.localizeText('DataReadError', [binding.OrderS]));
    });

    clientAPI.dismissActivityIndicator();
    return clientAPI.executeAction("/App/Actions/ShowSalesOrderActions/NavToShowSalesOrder.action");

The binding transfers correctly to the Next Page.

entry.title: Re: Badi MD_EXT_SUP is not triggered in MD01n - MRP Live
entry.content:
For MD01 have used BADI MD_EXT_SUP / Method IF_EX_MD_EXT_SUP~GET_SUPPLYING_SL

For MD01n transaction resolved this by using the BADI PPH_MRP_RUN_BADI / Method  IF_PPH_MRP_RUN_BADI~PURREQ_BEFORE_UPDATE_ADJUST

Code for MD01n Badi Imp :

  METHOD if_pph_mrp_run_badi~purreq_before_update_adjust
        BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
        OPTIONS READ-ONLY
        USING ztl_gbl_param.

*  Data Declaration
    DECLARE lv_rows         INTEGER;
    DECLARE lv_doc_type     CHAR(4);
    DECLARE lv_issuing_sloc CHAR(4);

* Execute logic only If CT_EBAN_NEW is not initial
    lv_rows = record_count( :ct_eban_new );
    IF lv_rows <> 0 THEN
* Purchase Requisition Type -
   lv_doc_type = 'XXXX'.

* Issuing Storage Location --> Fetch Issuing Storage Location from the GLobal Parameters Table
  lv_issuing_sloc = 'ZXXX'

* Check 'XXXX' document exists or not --> Execute custom logic only if entry is present
    SELECT COUNT (*) INTO lv_rows FROM :ct_eban_new WHERE bsart = :lv_doc_type;
    If lv_rows <> 0 THEN

* Default Issuing Storage location to 'XXXX' ONLY for the ZXXX Document Types
    ct_eban_new = SELECT a.the_index, a.doc_key_tmp, a.bnfpo, a.bsart,
                         a.bstyp, a.bsakz, a.loekz, a.statu, a.estkz,
                         a.frgkz, a.frgzu, a.frgst, a.ekgrp, a.ernam,
                         a.erdat, a.afnam, a.lastchangedatetime, a.creationdate,
                         a.creationtime, a.txz01, a.matnr, a.ematn,
                         a.werks, a.lgort, a.bednr, a.matkl,
                         a.reswk, a.menge, a.meins, a.bumng,
                         a.badat, a.lpein, a.lfdat, a.frgdt,
                         a.webaz, a.preis, a.peinh, a.pstyp,
                         a.knttp, a.kzvbr, a.kflag, a.vrtkz,
                         a.twrkz, a.wepos, a.weunb, a.repos,
                         a.lifnr, a.flief, a.ekorg, a.vrtyp,
                         a.konnr, a.ktpnr, a.infnr, a.zugba,

                         a.revno, a.blckt, a.beswk, a.eprofile,
                         a.eprefdoc, a.eprefitm, a.gmmng, a.wrtkz,
                         :lv_issuing_sloc as reslo, a.kblnr,
                         a.kblpos, a.prio_urg, a.prio_req,
                         a.memorytype, a.anzsn, a.mhdrz, a.iprkz,
                         a.nodisp, a.srm_contract_id, a.srm_contract_itm,
                         a.budget_pd, a.eldat, a.iuid_relevant,
                         a.sgt_scat, a.sgt_rcat, a.fsh_season_year,
                         a.fsh_season, a.fsh_collection, a.fsh_theme
                         FROM :ct_eban_new as a
                         WHERE a.bsart = :lv_doc_type
                         UNION
                         SELECT a.the_index, a.doc_key_tmp, a.bnfpo, a.bsart,
                         a.bstyp, a.bsakz, a.loekz, a.statu, a.estkz,
                         a.frgkz, a.frgzu, a.frgst, a.ekgrp, a.ernam,
                         a.erdat, a.afnam, a.lastchangedatetime, a.creationdate,
                         a.creationtime, a.txz01, a.matnr, a.ematn,
                         a.werks, a.lgort, a.bednr, a.matkl,
                         a.reswk, a.menge, a.meins, a.bumng,
                         a.badat, a.lpein, a.lfdat, a.frgdt,
                         a.webaz, a.preis, a.peinh, a.pstyp,
                         a.knttp, a.kzvbr, a.kflag, a.vrtkz,
                         a.twrkz, a.wepos, a.weunb, a.repos,
                         a.lifnr, a.flief, a.ekorg, a.vrtyp,
                         a.konnr, a.ktpnr, a.infnr, a.zugba,
                         a.qunum, a.qupos, a.dispo, a.sernr,
                         a.bvdat, a.batol, a.bvdrk, a.ebeln,
                         a.ebelp, a.bedat, a.bsmng, a.lblni,
                         a.bwtar, a.xoblr, a.ebakz, a.rsnum,
                         a.sobkz, a.arsnr, a.arsps, a.fixkz,
                         a.bmein, a.revlv, a.vorab, a.packno,
                         a.kanba, a.bpueb, a.cuobj, a.frggr,
                         a.frgrl, a.aktnr, a.charg, a.umsok,
                         a.verid, a.fipos, a.fistl, a.geber,
                         a.kzkfg, a.satnr, a.mng02, a.dat01,
                         a.attyp, a.adrnr, a.adrn2, a.kunnr,
                         a.emlif, a.lblkz, a.kzbws, a.waers,
                         a.idnlf, a.gsfrg, a.mprof, a.kzfme,
                         a.spras, a.techs, a.mfrpn, a.mfrnr,
                         a.emnfr, a.fordn, a.fordp, a.plifz,
                         a.berid, a.uzeit, a.fkber, a.grant_nbr,
                         a.memory, a.banpr, a.rlwrt, a.blckd,
                         a.revno, a.blckt, a.beswk, a.eprofile,
                         a.eprefdoc, a.eprefitm, a.gmmng, a.wrtkz,
                         a.reslo as reslo, a.kblnr,
                         a.kblpos, a.prio_urg, a.prio_req,
                         a.memorytype, a.anzsn, a.mhdrz, a.iprkz,

                         a.budget_pd, a.eldat, a.iuid_relevant,

entry.title: How to Use cds.test() for Testing On-Premise Connections with Jest in SAP BAS?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm working on a CAP project in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) where I need to run automated tests (cds.test()) involving connections to on-premise environments using Jest.

Specifically, I want to:

  1. Configure the cds.test() environment to connect to on-premise systems securely.
  2. Simulate a hybrid setup where some services use on-premise data while others remain in the cloud.
  3. Run the tests locally in BAS while respecting the connection settings for both environments.

Here's an example of a test where I attempt a POST request to an OData service connected to ECC and validate the error response:

 

 

it('POST ECC', async () => {
    try {
        // where the connection to ECC is made
        const { status } = await POST('/odata/v4/<Service>/<EntitySet>',
            {
                CNPJ: '1234',
                Pedido: '1234'
            },
            {
                headers: {
                    'Authorization': authHeader
                }
            });
        expect(status).toEqual(201); //code 400 Failed to destination
});

 

 

Has anyone successfully configured and run such a setup? Any guidance, best practices, or examples would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your help! 😊

entry.title: Re: Creating a Dynamic Formatted Search Query for Send Me...
entry.content:
The Formatted Search (FMS) in the Subject field contained the variable $[$10.V_0], which successfully retrieved the document number from the context in which it was executed. I also tested the variable $[$10.V_1], and it correctly fetched the Object Type.

The test code is below:

SELECT concat($[$10.V_1], ': ', $[$10.V_0])

entry.title: Re: The behavior of "DATA()=0" for booked and unbooked data
entry.content:
Hi @N1kh1l 

DATA() = 0 * 0 & DATA() = 1 * 0  are same behavior with DATA() = 0 + 0 (calculation result). The value of the booked and unbooked cell has been changed to zero.

Moonjun

entry.title: Use of Average full time equivalent of Accrual period in Time Management to average the quotas
entry.content:
Hi All,

 

REQUIREMENT: There is a situation where in given time account year example: 1st Jan 2024 till 31st December 2024 there can be several records where employee moves from full time to part time or vice versa. System should automatically average the quotas based on type of employment.

EXAMPLE: Employee A worked as a full-time employee from 1st Jan till 31st March that is FTE=1 , and from 1st April till 31st October worked as part time that is FTE=0.5 and again November and December as Full Time.

CALCULATION :

Employement Type

Months

Number of Calendars days

FTE

No of Working days(Number of calendar days * FTE)

Quota

Full Time

1st Jan - 31st March

91

1

91

20

Part Time

1st Apr-31st October

214

0.5

107

20

Full Time

1st Nov-31st December

61

1

61

20

 

 

366 Days

 

259Days

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Final Calculation= 259/366*20=14.15

 

 

 

 

 

SOLUTION: STEP1

Firstly, we need to use the function Average full time equivalent for Accruable period () and store it in a variable as per the screenshot below:

In the fields mentioned please fill the details as per the requirement here I need the calculation for current account period hence I am using account valid from and account valid until.

STEP2: Use this variable under create time account details where it will be used to calculate the accruals.

STEP3: Also please make sure that under time account type, Please select the fields which will trigger recalculation.

Thanks and Regards,

Suraj

entry.title: 10 Key Updates to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Best Practices in 2H 2024 Release
entry.content:
The second half of 2024 brings exciting updates to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Best Practices, which include enhancements across Employee Central Core, Global Benefits, Position Management, and Contingent Workforce Management. This latest release focuses on enhanced content, better user experience, new validations, and improved workflows. Let’s explore some key new features and how these changes will make the best practices content more relevant to our customers.

1. Reimagined Benefit Enrollment for Pensions

Continuing with the migration of USA Benefits from the legacy enrollment experience to the reimagined enrollment experience, Pension plans are included in the new experience in the 2H 2024 release. This means employees now have an intuitive, user-friendly interface when enrolling in pension plans, helping them make informed decisions about their retirement benefits. This change leaves us with the ‘Allowances’ and ‘Deductible Allowances’ benefits types that are still in the legacy experience—these will be included in the reimagined experience in the coming releases.

2. Employee Central Quick Actions for Employee and Manager Self-Services

Quick actions save time by allowing employees and managers to access frequently needed updates directly. This update introduces quick action templates, including:

  • Change Contract End Date
  • View Location
  • Change Chosen Name

These options allow employees and managers to make straightforward updates in an independent manner.

3. New Validation for New Hire's Age in Germany

A validation has been added for German hires to check whether a new hire is over fourteen years of age. This age validation ensures that organizations can meet their employment age requirements automatically out-of-the-box.

4. Field Criteria to Legal Entity in Position Object

In the Position object, the Cost Center field now includes the field criteria for Legal Entity, allowing cost center data to be filtered by the assigned legal entity. This feature enables more precise data maintenance and makes it easier for organizations to manage cost allocations across different legal entities. This update improves the integrity of HR and internal accounting practices for global companies with multiple legal entities.

5. Changes to Approvers in the Position Workflows

The Position Approval Workflows now incorporate the dynamic role “SFCC Org Design Administrator.” The base object for this dynamic role has changed from ‘Job Information’ to ‘Position,’ meaning organizations can assign dynamic groups to this role more flexibly. This change simplifies the workflows, allowing local approvers to be added as necessary and streamlining the position approval process. This change is an improvement over previous version and ensures that approval workflows align with the organizational design requirements.

6. Beneficiaries for the Latest Enrollment Experience

This enhancement allows the addition of beneficiaries in the new Reimagined Benefits enrollment process for the USA Benefits. With this update, employees can now configure beneficiaries for insurance, pension, and savings plans in real-time as they complete their benefits selections. This integrated approach simplifies the enrollment experience and improves transparency for employees.

7. New Opt-Out Feature

Offering employees the flexibility to opt out of specific benefits can be crucial for tailoring benefits to individual needs. The setting “Allow Waiving of Benefit” at the benefit level is maintained to ensure that employees can now opt out of specific benefits in the Reimagined Benefits Enrollment experience for the USA. This flexibility allows employees to avoid unnecessary benefits that may not suit their needs, offering a more personalized benefits experience.

8. Concurrent Employment Workflow Change

For organizations managing concurrent employees, simplifying workflows is essential to ensure efficient approvals and minimize delays. Previously, concurrent employment creation triggered an approval workflow for the target manager, which could have created bottlenecks. Now, based on the feedback we received, the system only sends a notification to the source manager, streamlining the process and improving efficiency in managing concurrent employment scenarios.

9. Validation for Duplicdate Work Order IDs

Based on the feedback we got, there was a need to identify scenarios where an already-existing Work Order ID was used. With 2H 2024, a new validation triggers an error if an existing Work Order ID is entered for a contingent worker. This update helps prevent duplicate entries, ensuring data accuracy. A post-processing step in the Contingent Workforce Management Quick Guide now includes detailed instructions for business rules to assist implementation partners.

10. Sample Positions Have Country/Region Field Maintained

The Sample Employee Master Data provides demo organizational and employee data for running HR Business Processes in Employee Central. The sample data allows HR teams and implementation partners to test configurations, workflows, and integrations in a realistic environment, reducing the time needed to identify and resolve potential issues before going live. In the 2H 2024 release, we added the country/region data to all the sample positions. This change fixed an issue where some data could not be selected depending on the Country. e.g. Employee Class.

 

PS: This blog post does not cover the Time Management content in SAP Best Practices. You can review the What’s New Viewer for more details.

entry.title: Re: Easy ECC deployment
entry.content:
To help you with an easier deployment of SAP ECC and to analyze how effectively your modules and custom Z-codes are being used, there are a few tools and value accelerators that can make the process smoother and provide the insights you need:

1. SAP Readiness Check

  • What It Does: This tool is great for evaluating your current SAP ECC system before transitioning to S/4HANA. However, it’s also very handy for understanding the usage of your custom Z-codes and active modules in your system.
  • How It Helps:
    • It identifies custom code (including those pesky Z-codes) to show what's in use and what might be outdated or redundant.
    • You’ll get insights on active modules, compatibility issues, and even recommendations on optimization.
    • It provides a clear dashboard to see the big picture at a glance.

2. SAP Signavio Process Intelligence (Process Mining)

  • What It Does: This tool gives you a powerful way to analyze business processes within your SAP ECC system, helping you see what's being used effectively and what's gathering dust.
  • How It Helps:
    • It connects to your SAP ECC data to analyze business processes in real-time.
    • You can identify bottlenecks, underutilized custom developments, and redundant modules.
    • With this level of visibility, you can quickly pinpoint which Z-codes or modules are adding real value and which ones might need a re-think.
    • Use Plug and Gain Approach (Value Accelerator withing Signavio) with Signavio Process Insights and Process Intelligence:
      • This approach offers a quantitative analysis of your existing system and transaction codes.
      • You'll also get a detailed view of process execution, helping you understand how transactions are flowing and which areas are delivering the most value.

3. ABAP Code Inspector and Usage Analytics Tools

  • What It Does: These tools are designed to help you see which Z-codes and custom developments are genuinely adding value and which ones aren’t getting used as much.
  • How It Helps:
    • Tools like the ABAP Call Monitor (SCMON) can track what custom code is actually being executed.
    • Custom Code Lifecycle Management (CCLM) within SAP Solution Manager helps you catalogue and assess your custom objects to see how frequently they’re being used.

4. SAP Solution Manager Usage Analytics

  • What It Does: This provides a broader look at module utilization and helps you track and evaluate the performance of your custom developments.
  • How It Helps:
    • It offers insights into which modules are being used, how often, and how they align with your business goals.
    • You can keep tabs on key business process metrics to ensure you’re getting the most out of your system.

A Few More Tips:

  • If you're thinking about the move to S/4HANA, consider using Fiori apps for a more streamlined experience and better process optimization.
  • You might also find value in reaching out to SAP Enterprise Support Services. They can provide tailored guidance and tools to make sure you’re getting the most out of your current system and future plans.

With these tools and strategies, you'll have a clear view of what’s working, what’s not, and where you can make improvements for maximum impact. Happy optimizing

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager SAP Signavio Process Insights
